description: EXO Syntaxis visual builder node registry. Maps each node type to its EXOCHAIN Rust implementation, combinator algebra term, and required constitutional invariants.

identity-verify.label: Identity Verify
identity-verify.description: Verify a DID identity against the identity registry with cryptographic proof.

consent-request.label: Consent Request
consent-request.description: Request bailment consent from a principal for a specific scope and duration.

consent-verify.label: Consent Verify
consent-verify.description: Verify that active bailment consent exists for the requested operation.

consent-revoke.label: Consent Revoke
consent-revoke.description: Revoke previously granted bailment consent with immediate effect.

authority-check.label: Authority Check
authority-check.description: Verify that the actor has the required authority for the operation via delegation chain.

authority-delegate.label: Authority Delegate
authority-delegate.description: Delegate a subset of authority to another actor with attenuation.

kernel-adjudicate.label: Kernel Adjudicate
kernel-adjudicate.description: Submit an action request to the immutable kernel for constitutional adjudication.

invariant-check.label: Invariant Check
invariant-check.description: Evaluate a specific constitutional invariant against the current context.

combinator-sequence.label: Sequence
combinator-sequence.description: Execute child nodes in order. Each output feeds as input to the next.

combinator-parallel.label: Parallel
combinator-parallel.description: Execute child nodes independently and merge all outputs.

combinator-choice.label: Choice
combinator-choice.description: Try child nodes in order. First success wins.

combinator-guard.label: Guard
combinator-guard.description: Only proceed if predicate evaluates to true.

combinator-transform.label: Transform
combinator-transform.description: Apply a transformation function to the output of the inner combinator.

governance-propose.label: Governance Propose
governance-propose.description: Submit a governance proposal to the legislative branch for deliberation.

governance-vote.label: Governance Vote
governance-vote.description: Cast a vote on an active governance proposal.

governance-resolve.label: Governance Resolve
governance-resolve.description: Resolve a governance proposal after quorum is met.

escalation-trigger.label: Escalation Trigger
escalation-trigger.description: Trigger constitutional escalation when an invariant violation is detected.

human-override.label: Human Override
human-override.description: Emergency human intervention point. Always available per constitutional requirement.
human-override.combinator: Choice([Guard(Identity, Predicate { required_key: 'human_approved' }), Guard(Identity, Predicate { required_key: 'human_rejected' })])

dag-append.label: DAG Append
dag-append.description: Append a causally-ordered event to the immutable DAG ledger.

proof-generate.label: Proof Generate
proof-generate.description: Generate a cryptographic proof for a constitutional claim.

proof-verify.label: Proof Verify
proof-verify.description: Verify a cryptographic proof against its claim.

tenant-isolate.label: Tenant Isolate
tenant-isolate.description: Enforce tenant boundary isolation, ensuring operations cannot cross tenant boundaries without explicit delegation.

mcp-enforce.label: MCP Enforce
mcp-enforce.description: Enforce Model Context Protocol rules on an AI system interaction.
mcp-enforce.combinator: Sequence([Guard(Identity, Predicate { required_key: 'mcp_context_valid' }), Guard(Identity, Predicate { required_key: 'mcp_rules_satisfied' })])

combinator_algebra.description: The combinator algebra provides a deterministic composition language for governance operations.
terms: Sequence([a, b, c]) — execute in order, threading output to input
Parallel([a, b, c]) — execute independently, merge outputs
Choice([a, b, c]) — try in order, first success wins
Guard(inner, predicate) — proceed only if predicate holds
Transform(inner, fn) — modify the output of inner
Retry(inner, policy) — retry with backoff policy

invariants.SeparationOfPowers: No single actor may hold legislative + executive + judicial power
invariants.ConsentRequired: Action denied without active bailment consent
invariants.NoSelfGrant: An actor cannot expand its own permissions
invariants.HumanOverride: Emergency human intervention must always be possible
invariants.KernelImmutability: Kernel configuration cannot be modified after creation
invariants.AuthorityChainValid: Authority chain must be valid and unbroken
invariants.QuorumLegitimate: Quorum decisions must meet threshold requirements
invariants.ProvenanceVerifiable: All actions must have verifiable provenance
